advantages: O(1) insert/remove at the head, tail, or any known-position node.
No resize copies — capacity grows one node at a time.
Enables O(1) move-to-front / evict-tail patterns (LRU caches).
Doubly-linked variants make bidirectional traversal trivial.
disadvantages: O(n) random access — there's no index, only a chain to walk.
Higher per-element memory overhead (every node carries pointers).
Poor CPU cache locality — nodes are scattered across the heap.
Hand-rolled linked lists in Python are almost always slower than a plain list; reach for deque / OrderedDict instead.
